message:
test(hook): match shell-quoted env value in CARGO_TARGET_DIR rewrite assertion

`join_exec_command` emits its env-prefix tokens through shell-quoting,
which means a `KEY=VALUE` token comes back as `KEY='VALUE'` (single-
quoted around the value half) so the joined string round-trips
through any shell without re-interpreting characters in the value.

The test for `rewrite_cargo_target_dir_command_for_remote` (added
alongside the CARGO_TARGET_DIR sync rework in 41eb319) was asserting
the un-quoted form:

    "env RUST_BACKTRACE=1 cargo build --release"

which is what you'd write by hand, but not what `join_exec_command`
produces. Updates the expectation to:

    "env 'RUST_BACKTRACE=1' cargo build --release"

Behavior of the function under test is unchanged; only the test's
expected string is adjusted to match `join_exec_command`'s actual
output. The companion assertion that the rewritten command no
longer contains the stripped `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/...`
prefix is preserved.
